findings.description: The heading labels the entire block a 'verbatim framework', and every sibling rule in this cluster is hard_constraint+must. This rule alone is should_constraint+must, an internally inconsistent hardness/polarity pairing and inconsistent with the section's stated verbatim requirement for the CTA line.

Rule captures the support-program name and channel list but omits governance facets present in the source text, e.g. what 'PAL' means/authorizes and any scope limits on Authorization Support usage. Without these facets the rule cannot be enforced or audited against the source unit's implied compliance scope.

Single rule conflates two distinct governance concerns: (a) verbatim program name 'My Compass Support' and (b) the scope/description of services (PAL channels, Authorization Support). These have different verbatim-strictness needs and should be split so the name can be a strict copy_editorial rule while the scope description can carry different (looser) editorial flexibility.

Rule merges two separately-labeled source statements ('MOA primary' and 'MOA alt') into one 'approved_variants' rule without capturing any selection/usage guidance distinguishing when each variant applies, risking loss of the primary-vs-alternate distinction present in the source labeling.

Class is set to copy_editorial, but the unit describes a support-program's service scope (communication channels, authorization support) rather than pure verbatim marketing copy; class should better reflect a program/reference type distinct from the strict verbatim copy rules in this cluster.
